description: Length-matched V2 facts for algebra/topology conjectures. Distractors lengthened to match truth detail level.

facts.context: The Poincaré Conjecture for 3-manifolds stated that:
facts.truth: every simply connected closed 3-manifold is homeomorphic to S³ (proven by Perelman 2003)
distractors: every simply connected closed 3-manifold admits a flat Riemannian metric (still unresolved)
simply connected closed 3-manifolds can be homeomorphic to S³ or to RP³ (proven false by Smale)
every simply connected closed 3-manifold is diffeomorphic to S² × S¹ (refuted by Thurston)

facts.context: The smooth Poincaré conjecture in dimension 4 asks:
facts.truth: whether every smooth 4-manifold homeomorphic to S⁴ is diffeomorphic to S⁴ (open)
distractors: whether every topological 4-manifold admits a unique PL triangulation (resolved by Kirby)
whether smooth structures on S⁴ form a countably infinite group under connected sum (proven)
whether every smooth 4-manifold homeomorphic to CP² is diffeomorphic to CP² (refuted)

facts.context: Exotic R⁴s are smooth structures on R⁴ not diffeomorphic to standard R⁴. It is known that:
facts.truth: uncountably many exotic R⁴s exist (unique to dimension 4)
distractors: exactly 28 exotic smooth structures on R⁴ exist (analogous to S⁷)
exotic smooth structures on Rⁿ exist in every dimension n ≥ 4
only finitely many exotic R⁴s exist (bounded by Betti numbers)

facts.context: The unknotting problem asks if an algorithm exists to determine if a knot diagram represents the unknot:
facts.truth: is decidable but optimal complexity is unknown (known to be in NP ∩ co-NP)
distractors: is decidable and known to be solvable in deterministic polynomial time O(n³)
is undecidable in general, reducible to the halting problem via braid groups
is decidable but proven to require exponential time in the crossing number

facts.context: The Jacobian Conjecture states that a polynomial map F: Cⁿ → Cⁿ with constant nonzero Jacobian:
facts.truth: has a polynomial inverse (unproven for n ≥ 2)
distractors: has a rational but not necessarily polynomial inverse (proven by Abhyankar)
is proven to be bijective with polynomial inverse for all n (by Keller 1939)
has an analytic inverse that is polynomial only when deg(F) ≤ 3 (partial result)

facts.context: The Invariant Subspace Problem asks if every bounded linear operator on Hilbert space:
facts.truth: has a non-trivial closed invariant subspace (open for general operators)
distractors: has a non-trivial closed invariant subspace (proven true for all bounded operators by Enflo)
has been resolved: counterexamples on separable Hilbert space exist (Read 1985)
has a non-trivial invariant subspace only for compact and normal operators (proven)

facts.context: The chromatic number of the plane asks how many colors are needed so no two points at distance 1 share a color:
facts.truth: is between 5 and 7 (de Grey 2018 proved ≥5, upper bound 7 known)
distractors: is exactly 4 (proven by a computer-assisted exhaustive search in 2019)
is exactly 7 (proven by extending the four-color theorem to infinite graphs)
is between 4 and 6 (Nelson 1950 proved ≥4, Soifer 2003 proved ≤6)

facts.context: The Hadamard Conjecture states that Hadamard matrices exist for:
facts.truth: every order divisible by 4 (unproven; smallest unknown order is 668)
distractors: every order that is a prime power times 4 (proven by Paley construction)
all positive even orders (proven using generalized Sylvester construction)
every order divisible by 4 (proven up to order 1000 by exhaustive computation)

facts.context: Schanuel's Conjecture in transcendental number theory states that if z₁,...,zₙ are linearly independent over Q:
facts.truth: the transcendence degree of Q(z₁,...,zₙ,e^z₁,...,e^zₙ) over Q is at least n
distractors: the transcendence degree of Q(z₁,...,zₙ,e^z₁,...,e^zₙ) over Q is exactly 2n
the numbers e^z₁,...,e^zₙ are all algebraically independent over Q(z₁,...,zₙ)
the transcendence degree of Q(e^z₁,...,e^zₙ) over Q is at most n−1 (Baker bound)

facts.context: The Sullivan Conjecture (proven) states that for finite group G and finite-dimensional CW complex X:
facts.truth: the space of pointed maps from BG to X is weakly contractible (Miller 1984)
distractors: the space of pointed maps from BG to X has finitely many connected components (Lannes 1987)
the space of pointed maps from BG to X is weakly contractible only for abelian G (Miller 1984)
the space of unpointed maps from BG to X is weakly contractible for all CW complexes (Sullivan 1970)
